talk_metadata.talk_title: Induced proximity approaches for targeting transcription factors and epigenetic reprogramming
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we see loss of MYC at two to four hours, but it's basically recovered by six hours because MYC has such high turnover rates"
Speaker said: "we still see off‑target engagement – the original hit molecule has over 100 off targets, so that's quite promiscuous still"
Speaker said: "we don't see global screwing up of transcription, we see quite selective manipulation of MYC target genes"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Rapid MYC protein turnover limits sustained degradation
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: we see loss of MYC at two to four hours, but it's basically recovered by six hours because MYC has such high turnover rates

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: High off‑target reactivity (>100 proteins) reduces selectivity

competitive_moat_weakness.vulnerable_ip_or_chemistry: Covalent sulfonyl zeradine oxindole scaffold for MYC
competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker disclosed that the scaffold still engages >100 off‑targets, indicating limited novelty of the core chemistry
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Reliance on covalent warhead may limit broader applicability to other TFs

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed stereoselective engagement of MYC indicating a transient pocket, then concluded that "degradation may not even be the best way to disrupt transcription factors" despite having a functional degrader

key_data_gaps: No in vivo efficacy data for MYC degraders
No toxicity or safety data presented

crispro_opportunity.description: CrisPRO could model the transient pocket dynamics of intrinsically disordered MYC regions to design more selective covalent warheads with fewer off‑targets.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted that MYC cysteine 203 is hyperreactive in a transient pocket and that off‑target promiscuity remains high.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Use computational simulations of IDR ensembles to predict pocket formation and guide warhead placement, reducing off‑target reactivity.
external_validation_needed: Structural NMR data of MYC IDR ensembles

crispro_opportunity.description: Identify tumors with low MYC turnover or co‑expression of stabilizing partners where covalent degraders would be more effective.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker highlighted rapid MYC turnover as a challenge for sustained degradation.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Integrate CrisPRO predictive models with transcriptomic data to flag patients likely to benefit from MYC covalent degraders.
external_validation_needed: Clinical datasets linking MYC half‑life to treatment response
Validation of turnover biomarkers (e.g., ubiquitination signatures)

unresolved_questions: Can stereoselective covalent binders achieve durable MYC suppression without frequent re‑synthesis?
What structural features of the neighboring aspartic acid enable selective reactivity?

assets_to_track: MYC covalent degrader programs at UC Berkeley

talk_metadata.talk_title: RIP‑Tag platform: AR‑BRD4 and ER‑BRD4 induced proximity therapeutics
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we see activity across heterogeneous mechanisms of resistance"
Speaker said: "we have a very weak binary interaction with the essential protein in healthy tissues"
Speaker said: "we never saw a hook effect preclinically"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Potential off‑target BRD4 inhibition may cause hematologic/hepatic effects not fully characterized
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: activity observed in patients without AR amplification or with ARV7 splice variant
vulnerability_identified.hidden_tox_signal: grade 1 fatigue reported; no thrombocytopenia but limited safety data

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: AR expression‑driven sequestration of BRD4 leading to loss‑of‑function
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: No biomarker‑driven selection; enrolling all mCRPC patients regardless of AR status
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Quantitative AR protein expression threshold to enrich for responders
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Small patient numbers per dose cohort limit power to detect rare toxicities

competitive_moat_weakness.vulnerable_ip_or_chemistry: RIP‑Tag heterobifunctional scaffold linking tumor‑specific ligand to BRD4 binder
competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker disclosed crystal structures of ternary complexes, revealing that the BRD4 binding pocket is only weakly engaged, suggesting that alternative small molecules could compete
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Relies on tumor‑specific protein expression; loss of AR or ER could render the modality ineffective

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker presented potent ternary complex formation and tumor regressions, then stated that the modality does not require the target (AR/ER) to be a driver, which contradicts the observed dependence on full‑length AR/ER for activity

crispro_opportunity.description: Use CrisPRO to model AR/ER expression thresholds and predict which patients will achieve sufficient ternary complex formation.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted activity across AR‑amplified and non‑amplified patients but no quantitative biomarker used.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Integrate quantitative proteomics with CrisPRO binding models to define a minimal AR/ER expression cutoff for clinical response.
external_validation_needed: Retrospective analysis of AR/ER protein levels in trial cohorts
Prospective validation in a biomarker‑enriched arm

crispro_opportunity.description: Combine RIP‑Tag with agents that down‑regulate BRD4 compensatory pathways to prevent resistance.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker mentioned that resistance mechanisms upstream of transcription factors may still converge on MYC, suggesting potential escape routes.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Predict synthetic lethal interactions using CrisPRO network analysis to identify optimal combination partners.
external_validation_needed: In‑vivo combination studies with BET inhibitors

cited_competitors.name: PROTACs targeting BRD4
cited_competitors.context: Compared to traditional degraders, RIP‑Tag offers tumor‑selective sequestration

cited_competitors.name: Fulvestrant
cited_competitors.context: ER RIP‑Tag shown superior to fulvestrant in preclinical models

unresolved_questions: Will loss of AR or ER expression in tumors lead to rapid resistance?
What is the long‑term safety profile of chronic BRD4 sequestration?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Molecular glues, viral‑E3 (VIPER‑TAC) and LIMTAC platforms for induced proximity
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we have more than half a million molecular glue library"
Speaker said: "we never saw a hook effect preclinically"
Speaker said: "the toxicity profile seen are very minimal"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Gemin3 is pan‑essential; no therapeutic window
Dependence on viral E6 expression; heterogeneous in HPV‑positive tumors
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: you have to consider that the virus sometimes silences E6 in cancer cells

competitive_moat_weakness.vulnerable_ip_or_chemistry: DNA‑encoded molecular glue library on beads
competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker described the library composition, indicating that the core hydroxyproline scaffold is widely used, potentially limiting novelty
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: High reliance on screening throughput; translation to drug‑like candidates not yet demonstrated

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that VHL‑Gemin3 glue alters gene expression but then admitted the target is non‑selective and not therapeutically useful, contradicting the claim of a viable drug candidate

key_data_gaps: No in vivo efficacy or safety data for VIPER‑TAC or LIMTAC molecules
Lack of clinical biomarker validation for viral E6 expression

crispro_opportunity.description: Apply CrisPRO to redesign VHL‑Gemin3 glue to target a cancer‑specific partner instead of pan‑essential Gemin3, creating a selective therapeutic.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted that Gemin3 is essential in all cells, making the glue non‑viable as a drug.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Use CrisPRO to predict alternative ternary interfaces that retain VHL binding but swap Gemin3 for a tumor‑specific protein.
external_validation_needed: Structural modeling of VHL‑alternative protein interfaces
Cell viability screens with redesigned glues

crispro_opportunity.description: Incorporate HPV‑E6 expression testing as a mandatory enrollment biomarker for VIPER‑TAC trials to avoid enrolling patients lacking the viral protein.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker acknowledged heterogeneous E6 expression could limit efficacy.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Leverage CrisPRO expression datasets to define a quantitative E6 threshold for trial eligibility.
external_validation_needed: Retrospective analysis of HPV‑positive tumor cohorts
Development of a clinical assay for E6 protein levels

cited_competitors.name: Cereblon‑based PROTACs
cited_competitors.context: Compared to VHL‑based glues, cereblon is the traditional ligase used in degraders

cited_competitors.name: BET inhibitors
cited_competitors.context: Mentioned as standard BRD4 targeting agents versus RIP‑Tag approach

unresolved_questions: Can viral‑E3 ligase based VIPER‑TACs achieve sufficient selectivity in heterogeneous HPV‑positive tumors?
What is the durability of LIMTAC‑mediated KRAS degradation after drug washout in vivo?
